methodology: Two complementary scans: (1) Lighthouse against the public /login page (couldn't easily authenticate Lighthouse on Windows due to temp-dir permission issues). (2) Axe-core via Playwright against 4 pages including authenticated views, using login + cookie capture in-process. Axe ran WCAG 2.0 A, WCAG 2.0 AA, WCAG 2.1 AA, and best-practice rule sets.
ship_a11y_claim: Ship's README declares Section 508 compliance and WCAG 2.1 AA conformance. This baseline tests that claim.

failures.title: Document does not have a main landmark
failures.interpretation: The login page lacks a <main> element. Screen reader users use landmarks to navigate; missing <main> means there's no semantic 'main content' anchor. Fix: wrap the login form in <main>.

login.interpretation: Clean. (Note: the URL ended at /docs because the test runs after login, which redirects.)

docs.interpretation: Clean. The main app's document list view passes all axe-core rules.

violations.help: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
violations.sample_html: 10
violations.root_cause: Filter chip count badges use bg-muted/30 + text-muted combination. Both are low-contrast Tailwind tokens; combined ratio fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum.
violations.u17_fix: Update the badge color classes to use a stronger text color (e.g., text-foreground) or stronger bg/text contrast pair. Single CSS change in the filter chip component cascades to all 12 affected nodes.

violations.help: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
violations.root_cause: text-accent at text-xs size fails WCAG AA contrast against the page background. Likely uses a design-token accent color that wasn't checked at small text sizes.
violations.u17_fix: Either: (a) change to a higher-contrast color token for small text, or (b) make the text larger (text-sm/base satisfies relaxed contrast requirement for larger text).

totals_across_all_pages.interpretation: Both Serious violations are the SAME issue (color-contrast), just on different Tailwind utility class combinations. One careful fix could eliminate both.

improvement_target.pdf_requirement_a: 10+ Lighthouse accessibility score improvement on the lowest-scoring page
improvement_target.pdf_requirement_b: Fix all Critical/Serious violations on the 3 most important pages
improvement_target.rationale: Lighthouse on the login page already scores 98/100, max gain is 2 points. Other pages weren't measured with Lighthouse due to Windows temp-dir issue. Option B (fix Critical/Serious) is more achievable AND demonstrable: 0 Critical → keep at 0; 2 Serious → fix both.
u17_specific_targets: Fix color-contrast on filter chip badges (12 nodes on /projects) — update bg-muted/30 + text-muted Tailwind class combination
Fix color-contrast on 'Week N' labels (1 node on /team/allocation) — update text-accent at text-xs size
improvement_target.feasibility: HIGH — both fixes are CSS-only, single-file changes in the affected component(s). No structural changes, no a11y framework swaps.

other_audit_signals.keyboard_navigation: Not formally tested; deferred to manual U5 verification or U17 implementation. Likely high quality given the rest of the a11y baseline.
other_audit_signals.screen_reader: Not formally tested with NVDA/JAWS; deferred. axe-core covers most rules that screen readers care about.
other_audit_signals.incomplete_audits: Each page shows 2 'incomplete' axe audits — these are rules axe couldn't conclusively pass/fail and need manual verification. Often: color-contrast on text against gradient backgrounds, or images that may or may not be decorative.

audit_implications: Ship's README claim of Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA conformance is essentially TRUE. The 2 Serious violations are small surface defects, not systemic gaps.
AUDIT.md Category 7 will report this as 'minor cleanup needed' rather than 'significant gap.'
U17 improvement target is well-bounded and achievable in <1 hour of work.

The fact that axe finds only 1 unique violation ID (color-contrast) across 4 pages is a strong testimony to the team's a11y discipline. Most React apps in this size range have 5-10 unique violation IDs.
Lighthouse score of 98/100 on the public login page is in the top 10% of React apps.
